sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.50 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: At a conference in Oberwolfach in 1979 I exhibited a finitely generated soluble group$G$(of derived length 3) and a non-zero cyclic${ Z}G$-module$V$such that$V V V$. Can this be done with$G$metabelian? I conjecture that it cannot. For background of this problem and for details of the construction see (P.\,M.\,Neumann, in: Groups--Korea, Pusan, 1988$($Lect. Notes Math., 1398), Springer, Berlin, 198...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
